judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports independently verify the same facts (dead base, squashed #42, three-dot vs two-dot diff divergence, rebase plan with concrete re-verification) and hit all four expectations comparably well; Report 2 adds an explicit git merge-base --is-ancestor check, giving it a marginally more rigorous ancestry proof.

report_1.evidence: main. The weighted-scorer work that feat/pairwise-metric was built on top of has already landed on main ... there's no reason to target feat/weighted-scorer since that branch's work is already upstream

report_2.evidence: main. Per project documentation's dead-base rule, every PR should target the trunk, not another feature branch ... There is no reason to stack this PR on feat/weighted-scorer; its content is already on main.

report_1.evidence: feat/pairwise-metric ... was branched from the tip of feat/weighted-scorer (ac9d505 "W3: token-overlap helper in metrics") ... main advanced independently to 1971f08 "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)" ... feat/weighted-scorer is now dead/obsolete as a branch — its content is already in main and it should not be merged or targeted.

report_2.evidence: the weighted-scorer work feat/pairwise-metric depends on already landed on main, but it landed as an independent, presumably squashed/rewritten commit (PR #42) rather than by merging feat/weighted-scorer itself. feat/weighted-scorer is therefore a dead base — a real branch that still exists on the remote, but is superseded

report_1.evidence: The merge-base diff (git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric, which is what GitHub computes and would render in the PR): 21 lines across three files, additionally including the full scorer.py rewrite ... It's present purely as a side effect of stacking on an in-progress branch that later landed via squash-merge rather than fast-forward/rebase.

report_2.evidence: Three-dot (git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric) ... 3 files, 21 insertions, including the entire feat/weighted-scorer payload (the overlap() function, the WEIGHTS dict, and the rewritten scorer.py ...) ... carried along because feat/pairwise-metric was branched on top of it before it merged into main on its own path.

report_1.evidence: Rebase feat/pairwise-metric onto current main: git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric ... re-verify the diff is exactly the 5-line pairwise change: git diff main feat/pairwise-metric --stat should match git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric --stat once the branch is rebased

report_2.evidence: Rebase feat/pairwise-metric onto current main (git rebase main ... or git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric ...) ... re-verify the diff is clean and scoped: git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric should drop back to the 2-file/5-line diff ... and git log main..feat/pairwise-metric should show only the X1/X2-equivalent commit(s) with main's 1971f08 in its ancestry.
